module1.clean_text:
Hospitalization report
This is the patient [PATIENT] aged [AGE] originally from and residing in ORAN, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, followed at our level since [DATE] for cystic fibrosis with double respiratory and digestive phenotypic expression on the homozygous genetic mutation. F508del; Admitted to our department on [DATE] for an exacerbation of his illness. HISTORY:
Family:
-Father: aged [AGE] in BES. -Mother: aged [AGE] in BES. -Consanguinity: no
-Siblings: Brother aged [AGE] known from service for cystic fibrosis. Child died at 03 days of life due to lack of meconium emission. Personal:
Physiological
-Pregnancy: Well monitored
-Term birth by vaginal route. -Apgar [DATE] then [DATE] weight: 03kg300, Height (51 cm), BW (34 cm)
-Meconium emitted <24 hours, no notion of neonatal jaundice
- Breastfeeding: Exclusive maternal until the age of [AGE] then artificial
-Diversification started at the age of [AGE] CORRECT
-Psychomotor development: GOOD
-Schooling: 4th AM, good academic performance. Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestic:
Concept of delayed meconium emission. Death of a brother at the age of [AGE] (no meconium emission). Brother aged [AGE] known for cystic fibrosis. Clinic:
Chronic cough since the age of [AGE] congestion from the age of [AGE] oily diarrhea from the age of [AGE] sweat test:
1st Test on [DATE] forearm) 262 mg (sweat weight) 90 meq/l (chlorine level)
02nd Test [DATE] (Right forearm) 216 mg 93 meq/l
- Genetic study: [DATE] homozygous mutation p.F508del
-Fecal elastase [DATE] 1.8ug/ exocrine pancreatic insufficiency
Processing in progress:
-For respiratory purposes:
Respiratory physiotherapy at home twice daily
Hypertonic nebulization
Nebulization with Colistin twice a day every other month
- For nutritional purposes:
Creon 12000 IU (520 IU/Kg)
Vit K 10 mg/15 days
Vit D amp/3 months
Vit E 1cp/d
Therapeutic compliance: Good
HISTORY OF ILLNESS: related to the reason for hospitalization: 03/24/2026
The beginning of the signs dates back 15 days previously with accentuation of the cough becoming annoying, modification of the sputum which has become greenish and thick, with asthenia and reduction in appetite hence its admission. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date: 03/24/2026
• Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 45,800 kg (-0.81 DS). Height: 162 cm (+0.15 DS). BMI:17.5 kg/m2 (-1.04 SD). Eutrophic child
• Average general condition, febrile: T: 40.1° C, mild malnutrition. • Skin examination: Pale skin, no jaundice or hemorrhagic syndrome. • ENT examination: allergic rhinitis
• Pleuropulmonary examination: FR: 18 c/, fits of wet cough, auscultation, bilateral rumbling rales, vesicular murmurs well transmitted in 2 pulmonary fields. - No signs of struggle, Spo2: 97% under AA. - Signs of chronic respiratory failure (clubbing + Pectus Excavatum chest deformity)
• Cardiovascular examination: HR: 88b/min. Auscultation: no breathing or BSA, heart sounds were clearly audible. • Examination of the abdomen, distended soft abdomen, 01 to 02 soft fatty stools, no signs of dehydration, free hernia orifices, no rectal prolapse. • Reduced appetite, disturbed sleep. • Preserved diuresis. • Examination of other devices without particularity. PARACLINICAL ASSESSMENT UPON ADMISSION: 10/28/25
CBC: Coagulated blood
TP: 69.6% TQ: 15.6 Sec TCK: 33Sec
CRP: 7.9mg/l (negative). PCT: < 0.05 ng/ml (negative)
Blood ionogram (normal): Na: 138.9 mmol/l K: 3.89 mmol/l Cl: 102 mmol/l
Liver test (normal): AST: 11U/l gGT: 17.57 LDH: 333 U/L
Blood sugar: 0.97 g/L (normal). TG: 1.25 g/l (normal). Magnesemia: 21.95 ml (normal). Calcemia: 97.2 mg/L (normal)
Albumin: 38g/l (normal). Creat: 5.02 mg/l (normal). Urea: 0.12 g/l (normal). Total Protein: 69.3 g/l (normal)
Ferritin: 2.1 ng/ml (low). 25 OH Vit D: 28.3 ng/ml (slight deficiency)
SUPPORT:
On the respiratory level
Nebulization with SSH before respiratory physiotherapy. Twice-daily respiratory physiotherapy
Bi IV antibiotic therapy:
Fortum 200mg/kg/day. Amikacin 25 mg/kg/day IV for 10 days. Zomax 1 day/2 days
Nutritionally:
Interview with the dietitian. Growth curve.
Polyvitamin supplementation: {vitamin D/2 months Vit K/15days Vit E/15days} and iron deficiency. Creon 12000 iu 01 capsule 10/d
Venofer infusion. Evolution during hospitalization:
Regression of signs of struggle with complete disappearance of cough and reduction in the volume of sputum. The Child stayed for 13 days having received Bi antibiotic therapy: Fortum for 13 days, Amikacin for 10 days with venofer infusion 3 times at 3 day intervals and vitamin supplementation. OUTLINE AND FOCUS OF THE DISEASE:
On the respiratory level:
Sign of chronic respiratory failure “clubbing + Thoracic deformity”
No signs of chronic cor pulmonale. Heart echo (05/04//2026): without anomaly
EFR (01-12-24):
FEV 63% FEV/FVC 112%
FVC 57% DEP 90 L/sec
Decrease in all proximal and distal flow rates, restrictive ventilatory deficit
ECBC of sputum: 03/29/2026: Absence of germs (after course of antibiotics). Arterial blood gas: 06/16/25: Normal ph: 7.434 po2:91 mm hg pco2 39.5 mm hg
Thoracic CT 01/2025: Bilateral cystic dilatation of the bronchi with thickening of the bronchial walls, Right inter-bronchial mediatisnal lymphadenopathy of 11mm. On the hemodynamic level
No signs of heart failure (no edema or hepatosplenomegaly, or turgidity of the jugulars. No signs of chronic cor pulmonale. Heart echo 04/05/2026: Without abnormalities. On the digestive and hepatobiliary level:
No jaundice, no notion of digestive bleeding, hepatomegaly. Absence of constipation,
Absence of rectal prolapse. Correct liver test: AST: 11U/l gGT: 17.57 LDH: 333 U/L
Abdominopelvic ultrasound 04/06/2026: Homogeneous hepato-splenomegaly without detectable nodular lesion. Free intraperitoneal effusion of moderate abundance. Simple left ovarian cyst of 36mm. Nutritionally and metabolically:
Weight: 45,800 kg (-0.81 DS). Height: 162 cm (+0.15 DS). BMI:17.5 kg/m2 (-1.04 SD). No weight loss. Preserved daily ration
Blood sugar: 0.80 g/l HbA1C (12/24/2025): 6.30%. Correct nutritional assessment except hypovitaminosis D and hypoferritinemia
Ferritin: 2.1 ng/ml 25 OH Vit D: 28.3 ng/ml
Normal growth curve. On the ENT level:
Nasal obstruction, rhinorrhea
CT of the sinuses (03-23-2025): maxillo-sphenoethmoidal sinusitis (very dense content),
CONCLUSION:
The child Bendjlida Israa aged 14, originally from and residing in Oran, from a non-consanguineous couple, followed since the age of 3 months for cystic fibrosis with dual respiratory and digestive phenotypic expression, readmitted for moderate exacerbation of cystic fibrosis which had progressed well under treatment. Child discharged after good clinical and biological progress with control appointment on 07/07/2026
To collect / To do:
- Thoracic CT with injection
- ECBC with antibiogram
- Plan an HbA1C with OGTT
- Provide aspergillus serology
- Plan a nighttime saturation curve for the next hospitalization
- Referral to ENT
Resident of the department: Dr Djoudi Assistant of the department: Dr OUSSALAH

phenopacket.input:
Hospitalization report
This is the patient BENDJELIDA ISRAA, aged 14, originally from and residing in ORAN, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, followed at our level since December 2011 for cystic fibrosis with double respiratory and digestive phenotypic expression on the homozygous genetic mutation. F508del; Admitted to our department on 03/24/2026 for an exacerbation of his illness. HISTORY:
Family:
-Father: aged 62 in BES. -Mother: aged 42; in BES. -Consanguinity: no
-Siblings: Brother aged 09 known from service for cystic fibrosis. Child died at 03 days of life due to lack of meconium emission. Personal:
Physiological
-Pregnancy: Well monitored
-Term birth by vaginal route. -Apgar 7/10 then 09/10
-Birth weight: 03kg300, Height (51 cm), BW (34 cm)
-Meconium emitted <24 hours, no notion of neonatal jaundice
- Breastfeeding: Exclusive maternal until the age of 4 months then artificial
-Diversification started at the age of 6 months
-Vaccination: CORRECT
-Psychomotor development: GOOD
-Schooling: 4th AM, good academic performance. Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestic:
Concept of delayed meconium emission. Death of a brother at the age of 3 days (no meconium emission). Brother aged 09 known for cystic fibrosis. Clinic:
Chronic cough since the age of 1 month
Bronchial congestion from the age of 2 months
Chronic oily diarrhea from the age of 2 months
Paraclinic:
-Positive sweat test:
1st Test on 02/11/2011
(Right forearm) 262 mg (sweat weight) 90 meq/l (chlorine level)
02nd Test 11/29/2011 (Right forearm) 216 mg 93 meq/l
- Genetic study: 01/31/2012: homozygous mutation p.F508del
-Fecal elastase 03/26/2025: 1.8ug/ exocrine pancreatic insufficiency
Processing in progress:
-For respiratory purposes:
Respiratory physiotherapy at home twice daily
Hypertonic nebulization
Nebulization with Colistin twice a day every other month
- For nutritional purposes:
Creon 12000 IU (520 IU/Kg)
Vit K 10 mg/15 days
Vit D amp/3 months
Vit E 1cp/d
Therapeutic compliance: Good
HISTORY OF ILLNESS: related to the reason for hospitalization: 03/24/2026
The beginning of the signs dates back 15 days previously with accentuation of the cough becoming annoying, modification of the sputum which has become greenish and thick, with asthenia and reduction in appetite hence its admission. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date: 03/24/2026
• Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 45,800 kg (-0.81 DS). Height: 162 cm (+0.15 DS). BMI:17.5 kg/m2 (-1.04 SD). Eutrophic child
• Average general condition, febrile: T: 40.1° C, mild malnutrition. • Skin examination: Pale skin, no jaundice or hemorrhagic syndrome. • ENT examination: allergic rhinitis
• Pleuropulmonary examination: FR: 18 c/, fits of wet cough, auscultation, bilateral rumbling rales, vesicular murmurs well transmitted in 2 pulmonary fields. - No signs of struggle, Spo2: 97% under AA. - Signs of chronic respiratory failure (clubbing + Pectus Excavatum chest deformity)
• Cardiovascular examination: HR: 88b/min. Auscultation: no breathing or BSA, heart sounds were clearly audible. • Examination of the abdomen, distended soft abdomen, 01 to 02 soft fatty stools, no signs of dehydration, free hernia orifices, no rectal prolapse. • Reduced appetite, disturbed sleep. • Preserved diuresis. • Examination of other devices without particularity. PARACLINICAL ASSESSMENT UPON ADMISSION: 10/28/25
CBC: Coagulated blood
TP: 69.6% TQ: 15.6 Sec TCK: 33Sec
CRP: 7.9mg/l (negative). PCT: < 0.05 ng/ml (negative)
Blood ionogram (normal): Na: 138.9 mmol/l K: 3.89 mmol/l Cl: 102 mmol/l
Liver test (normal): AST: 11U/l gGT: 17.57 LDH: 333 U/L
Blood sugar: 0.97 g/L (normal). TG: 1.25 g/l (normal). Magnesemia: 21.95 ml (normal). Calcemia: 97.2 mg/L (normal)
Albumin: 38g/l (normal). Creat: 5.02 mg/l (normal). Urea: 0.12 g/l (normal). Total Protein: 69.3 g/l (normal)
Ferritin: 2.1 ng/ml (low). 25 OH Vit D: 28.3 ng/ml (slight deficiency)
SUPPORT:
On the respiratory level
Nebulization with SSH before respiratory physiotherapy. Twice-daily respiratory physiotherapy
Bi IV antibiotic therapy:
Fortum 200mg/kg/day. Amikacin 25 mg/kg/day IV for 10 days. Zomax 1 day/2 days
Nutritionally:
Interview with the dietitian. Growth curve.
Polyvitamin supplementation: {vitamin D/2 months Vit K/15days Vit E/15days} and iron deficiency. Creon 12000 iu 01 capsule 10/d
Venofer infusion. Evolution during hospitalization:
Regression of signs of struggle with complete disappearance of cough and reduction in the volume of sputum. The Child stayed for 13 days having received Bi antibiotic therapy: Fortum for 13 days, Amikacin for 10 days with venofer infusion 3 times at 3 day intervals and vitamin supplementation. OUTLINE AND FOCUS OF THE DISEASE:
On the respiratory level:
Sign of chronic respiratory failure “clubbing + Thoracic deformity”
No signs of chronic cor pulmonale. Heart echo (05/04//2026): without anomaly
EFR (01-12-24):
FEV 63% FEV/FVC 112%
FVC 57% DEP 90 L/sec
Decrease in all proximal and distal flow rates, restrictive ventilatory deficit
ECBC of sputum: 03/29/2026: Absence of germs (after course of antibiotics). Arterial blood gas: 06/16/25: Normal ph: 7.434 po2:91 mm hg pco2 39.5 mm hg
Thoracic CT 01/2025: Bilateral cystic dilatation of the bronchi with thickening of the bronchial walls, Right inter-bronchial mediatisnal lymphadenopathy of 11mm. On the hemodynamic level
No signs of heart failure (no edema or hepatosplenomegaly, or turgidity of the jugulars. No signs of chronic cor pulmonale. Heart echo 04/05/2026: Without abnormalities. On the digestive and hepatobiliary level:
No jaundice, no notion of digestive bleeding, hepatomegaly. Absence of constipation,
Absence of rectal prolapse. Correct liver test: AST: 11U/l gGT: 17.57 LDH: 333 U/L
Abdominopelvic ultrasound 04/06/2026: Homogeneous hepato-splenomegaly without detectable nodular lesion. Free intraperitoneal effusion of moderate abundance. Simple left ovarian cyst of 36mm. Nutritionally and metabolically:
Weight: 45,800 kg (-0.81 DS). Height: 162 cm (+0.15 DS). BMI:17.5 kg/m2 (-1.04 SD). No weight loss. Preserved daily ration
Blood sugar: 0.80 g/l HbA1C (12/24/2025): 6.30%. Correct nutritional assessment except hypovitaminosis D and hypoferritinemia
Ferritin: 2.1 ng/ml 25 OH Vit D: 28.3 ng/ml
Normal growth curve. On the ENT level:
Nasal obstruction, rhinorrhea
CT of the sinuses (03-23-2025): maxillo-sphenoethmoidal sinusitis (very dense content),
CONCLUSION:
The child Bendjlida Israa aged 14, originally from and residing in Oran, from a non-consanguineous couple, followed since the age of 3 months for cystic fibrosis with dual respiratory and digestive phenotypic expression, readmitted for moderate exacerbation of cystic fibrosis which had progressed well under treatment. Child discharged after good clinical and biological progress with control appointment on 07/07/2026
To collect / To do:
- Thoracic CT with injection
- ECBC with antibiogram
- Plan an HbA1C with OGTT
- Provide aspergillus serology
- Plan a nighttime saturation curve for the next hospitalization
- Referral to ENT
Resident of the department: Dr Djoudi Assistant of the department: Dr OUSSALAH
